full_prompt: Design a single monolithic balsa-wood tower structure for a seismic shake-table design challenge. The structure must be constructed only from balsa wood and rubber cement (no fasteners, metal joints, or non-balsa members), must fit within a rectangular plan area of 600 mm x 600 mm, and must be a minimum of 1500 mm tall and a maximum of 1650 mm tall. The tower supports a lumped 4.5 kg roof mass (sandbag simulator) and must survive prescribed shake-table ground motion time histories (scaled El Centro 1940 NS, PGA = 0.35 g; and scaled Northridge Sylmar NS, PGA = 0.60 g; each played for 30 s). Material: balsa wood, E = 3.5 GPa (longitudinal grain), density = 160 kg/m^3, compressive strength parallel to grain = 14 MPa, tensile strength parallel to grain = 20 MPa, shear strength = 2.0 MPa. Geometric constraints: base footprint <= 600 mm x 600 mm; height between 1500 and 1650 mm; uniform story height 300 mm creates 5-6 stories typical; no diagonal bracing may exit the 600 x 600 footprint. Load cases: (R1) Roof-mass static: 4.5 kg x 9.81 = 44 N vertical distributed across 4 roof joints; max compressive stress in base columns <= compressive strength / 2.0 = 7 MPa. (R2) El Centro scaled to PGA 0.35 g response-spectrum analysis applied at the base; peak roof acceleration <= 1.5 g; peak base-column axial stress <= 7 MPa; peak inter-story drift <= 0.02 x story height = 6 mm. (R3) Northridge Sylmar scaled to PGA 0.60 g; peak roof acceleration <= 2.5 g; peak base-column axial stress <= compressive strength / 1.5 = 9.3 MPa; peak inter-story drift <= 0.025 x story height = 7.5 mm. (R4) Total tower mass (scoring: structural efficiency) <= 250 g. (R5) Fundamental modal frequency hard-mounted at base shall be > 3 Hz (avoid resonance with El Centro dominant band 1-2 Hz). Primary verification: structural FEA linear static + response-spectrum or time-history dynamic analysis. Vibration (modal) secondary via R5. Thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 (or equivalent balsa-member assembly idealization), member schedule, and a report listing mass in g, peak roof acceleration per earthquake in g, max column stress in MPa, max inter-story drift in mm, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic balsa-wood tower for a seismic shake-table design challenge. Materials restricted to balsa wood and rubber cement (no fasteners, no metal, no non-balsa members). The tower supports a 4.5 kg lumped roof mass (sandbag simulator) and must survive prescribed shake-table motions: scaled El Centro 1940 NS at PGA 0.35 g and scaled Northridge Sylmar NS at PGA 0.60 g, each played for 30 s. Typical 5-6 stories at 300 mm story height; no diagonal bracing may exit the footprint.

Envelope: base footprint <= 600 x 600 mm, height 1500-1650 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 static roof mass: 4.5 kg (44 N) distributed across 4 roof joints.
- LC2 El Centro scaled to 0.35 g PGA: peak roof acceleration <= 1.5 g, peak inter-story drift <= 0.02 x story height = 6 mm.
- LC3 Northridge Sylmar scaled to 0.60 g PGA: peak roof acceleration <= 2.5 g, peak inter-story drift <= 0.025 x story height = 7.5 mm.
- LC4 modal: fundamental frequency > 3 Hz (out of the 1-2 Hz El Centro dominant band).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Seismic pass/fail is a dynamics problem; RS analysis or explicit time-integration is required beyond linear static.

prompt.title: Seismic design balsa tower
prompt.brief: Balsa-only tower, 1500-1650 mm tall, 600 x 600 footprint, supports 4.5 kg roof and survives El Centro 0.35 g + Northridge 0.60 g.

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Member sizing is stress and drift driven.
